# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 135

This is turn 135. Turns 1 through 134 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 135.

## Turn 135 — the cubic marked jet is Nielsen-trivial

Turn 133 introduced the marked \(h_0\)-repair, and Turn 134 proved that it preserves all first- and second-order coefficient data. 

Let \(F\) be the free pro-\(2\) group on \(\sigma,x_0,x_1\), with mod-\(2\) Zassenhaus filtration \(D_\bullet F\). Write

\[
r_{\rm old}=x_0^2x_1^3x_1^\sigma,\qquad
r_{\rm new}=x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0[x_1,\sigma].
\]

Using Magnus variables

\[
\sigma\mapsto1+A,\qquad x_0\mapsto1+B,\qquad x_1\mapsto1+C,
\]

both relators have quadratic term

\[
q_2=B^2+AC+CA.
\]

Their cubic terms are

\[
q_{3,\rm old}=A^2C+ACA+CAC+C^2A
\]

and

\[
q_{3,\rm new}=q_{3,\rm old}+A^2B+BA^2.
\]

The apparent new marked term is removable. Define the marked Nielsen automorphism

\[
\Theta(\sigma)=\sigma,\qquad
\Theta(x_0)=x_0,\qquad
\Theta(x_1)=x_1[x_0,\sigma].
\]

It fixes the unramified character exactly. Since the degree-two Magnus term of \([x_0,\sigma]\) is \(BA+AB\), its effect on \(q_2\) is

\[
A(BA+AB)+(BA+AB)A=A^2B+BA^2.
\]

A complete truncated multiplication gives the stronger identity

\[
\boxed{\Theta(r_{\rm old})\equiv r_{\rm new}\pmod{D_4F}.}
\]

Thus the old and repaired marked maximal pro-\(2\) groups have isomorphic **third Zassenhaus quotients**, despite the fact that their full marked groups are not isomorphic.

This also closes the proposed \(C_5\rtimes C_4\) and \(C_{17}\rtimes C_8\) tests at cubic depth uniformly. For

\[
T_{e,m}=C_e\rtimes C_{2^m},\qquad \operatorname{ord}_e(2)=2^m,
\]

let \(J_3(r)=F/\langle D_4F,r\rangle\), retain its unramified \(C_4\)-quotient, and set

\[
J_{3,m}(r)=J_3(r)\times_{C_4}C_{2^m}.
\]

Because \(\Theta\) fixes \(\sigma\), it induces

\[
J_{3,m}(r_{\rm old})\cong J_{3,m}(r_{\rm new})
\]

over \(C_{2^m}\), and therefore also an isomorphism after coupling to \(T_{e,m}\). This covers \(e=5,m=2\), \(e=17,m=3\), and every analogous tame quotient at once.

An exact enumeration gave

\[
|J_3(r_{\rm old})|=|J_3(r_{\rm new})|=2^{13},
\]

and both relators yield \(2^{23}\) generating triples with the same prescribed unramified \(C_4\)-frame.

The natural shear first fails one degree later. Modulo augmentation degree \(5\),

\[
\Theta(r_{\rm old})r_{\rm new}^{-1}=1+\Delta_4,
\]

where

\[
\begin{aligned}
\Delta_4={}&A^2B^2+B^2A^2+(AB+BA)^2+C^4\\
&+(AB+BA)(AC+CA)+(AC+CA)(AB+BA).
\end{aligned}
\]

So degree three is structurally incapable of validating the repair: the first potentially invariant marked obstruction is quartic. Whether \(\Delta_4\) survives all permitted degree-three Nielsen corrections remains open.

[Turn 135 cubic-jet certificate](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn135_cubic_jet_certificate.py)

**ESTABLISHED**: Computed the complete cubic Magnus jets. Proved that \(x_1\mapsto x_1[x_0,\sigma]\) carries the obsolete relation to the repaired marked relation modulo \(D_4\), so every third-jet tame coupling—including the entire \(\operatorname{ord}_e(2)=2^m\) family—is blind to the repair. Located the first residual term explicitly in degree four.

**GAP**: Determine whether the quartic residual is a genuine marked invariant or can be removed by a more general degree-three Nielsen transformation. The \(h_0\)-word remains a search candidate, not a presentation.

**NEXT**: Compute the full orbit of the quartic residual under all unramified-marking-preserving IA transformations through degree three. If its class survives, build one uniform fourth-jet pullback family \(T_{e,m}\times_{C_{2^m}}J_{4,m}\) that replaces both proposed isolated tame checks.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
